title: Router Decoupling
content:
# NodeJS-ExpressJS Interview Prep

## Q1: How does NodeJS resolve blocking I/O calls if JavaScript is single-threaded?

### Standard Answer
NodeJS delegates I/O calls to the kernel (using epoll/kqueue) or the background **libuv worker thread pool**. When an asynchronous call (such as a database query) is made, JavaScript registers a callback and halts execution. Once the background worker completes the query, it places the callback in the event loop queue, which is executed when the main thread call stack becomes empty.


## Q2: What is the spatial complexity bounds of Router Decoupling?

### Standard Answer
The spatial complexity scales strictly as $O(1)$ auxiliary memory unless additional arrays or dynamic heap nodes are allocated, yielding $O(N)$ total space.
